title: Alpha 6.2: Map Clustering & Bug Fixes
summary: Dense pin maps now cluster automatically, text labels can be rotated, and several map label bugs are fixed.
content:
Pin clustering

Map pins now cluster automatically when zoomed out. Click a cluster to zoom in and see individual pins. Clusters expand with a spider layout at maximum zoom.

Text label rotation

Text geometry features now support a rotation angle. Use the slider or number input in the inspector to rotate area labels to match your map's geography.

Bug fixes

  • Area label centering: Area name labels were losing their centering after collision detection ran, causing them to appear offset from their coat-of-arms badge. Fixed.
  • Panel toggle: The left-panel pull tab no longer slides on mousedown.
  • Pin labels after navigation: Clicking a pin in the Atlas panel now reliably shows its label after the map flies to it.
  • Area tooltip: Hovering over an area polygon no longer shows a redundant name tooltip.

Questions or feedback: support@taletrove.app.

title: Alpha 6: Session Layer
summary: GM session tracking, YouTube embeds, a unified left panel, and a round of UX polish.
content:
Session tracking for GMs

The left panel now has a dedicated SESSIONS section. Log play sessions directly from the panel, no article required. Atlas, Lore, and Sessions share one scrollable list.

What's new

  • Sessions panel: Create and manage play session records in the left panel. GM-only.
  • YouTube embeds: Paste a YouTube URL into an Embed block for an inline player. Good for ambience playlists stored inside your world.
  • Bug reporter: Use the bug icon in the nav rail to report issues or request features. Opens a pre-filled GitHub Issues form.
  • Appearance: Reworked theme and general settings with a live map preview.
  • UX cleanup: Tutorial steps, toolbar overlay menu, settings layout, and icon picker all tidied up.
  • Sample world: Aethermoor updated with richer articles, coat-of-arms areas, and a complete session log.

Questions or feedback: support@taletrove.app.

title: Welcome to TaleTrove Alpha
summary: TaleTrove is feature-complete for the Alpha. Build your world, explore its connections, and let us know what you think.
content:
Your world is ready to be built.

Welcome to TaleTrove - a local-first world-building atlas for game masters, writers, and world-builders. Everything you create lives on your device. No accounts, no subscriptions, no cloud required.

What's here

  • Atlas: Place pins, draw regions, and trace routes on any map image. Every feature can hold rich notes, images, and linked lore.
  • Encyclopedia: A structured library of People, Things, and Events - each one connected to the map and to each other through a typed link system.
  • Relationships: The Relational Graph and Family Tree views show the connections in your world at a glance.
  • Timeline: A calendar, vertical timeline, and Gantt view keep your world's history organised across any custom time system.
  • Google Drive Sync: Back up and open worlds directly from your own Google Drive. Your data stays yours.
  • Player Export: Share a sanitised version of your world with players, with GM-only content automatically stripped.
  • Obsidian Import: Already have a vault? Import your notes directly from an Obsidian vault folder - markdown content, frontmatter tags, and wiki-links all carry over into the Encyclopedia.

Alpha means feedback matters

TaleTrove is feature-complete for the Alpha outside of a small wishlist. The one big item still on the horizon is WebRTC multiplayer - real-time sharing so a whole table can explore the same world together.

Everything else is in your hands now. If something feels off, breaks, or could be better, we want to hear it. Reach us at support@taletrove.app - every report helps.

Go build something worth exploring.
